steps.title: Compare the slopes of both equations
steps.content: The first equation $y = x + 15$ has slope 1. The second equation $y = 5x$ has slope 5. Since the slopes are different, the lines are not parallel.

steps.title: Find the intersection point count
steps.content: When two lines have different slopes, they intersect at exactly one point. To verify, set the equations equal: $x + 15 = 5x$. Solving: $15 = 4x$, so $x = \frac{15}{4}$. This single solution confirms exactly one intersection point. That matches choice B — B is correct.
